response.justification: Maria Rodriguez has a broad software background and genuine geospatial exposure through GISCollective and Green Map, but the role demands strong modern C++ or Rust in production environments, and her C++ experience is limited to a 6-month stint at National Instruments over a decade ago plus a university thesis — neither of which reflects the depth or recency required. Her primary commercial stack is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, with no Rust experience and no evidence of routing tile pipelines, navigation guidance systems, or the low-level systems programming the role requires.
strengths: Geospatial domain familiarity through the GISCollective open-source Geo-Data platform and Green Map GIS application
Distributed systems exposure at Sociomantic building low-latency services handling millions of HTTP requests per second with async I/O
Demonstrated versatility picking up new stacks (mobile, embedded, web, data integration) across a long career
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and outdated — a 6-month role at NI and a bachelor thesis, with no mention of modern C++ in the skills list or recent work
No Rust experience anywhere in the resume
No routing tile pipeline, navigation guidance, or production geospatial data pipeline work at any employer

key_factors.factor: No routing or navigation pipeline experience
